description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from large, rectangular stone blocks forming a robust wall with a single, prominent arched opening at its center. The archway is supported by two thick, intricately patterned columns, each adorned with geometric reliefs and decorative motifs. The arch itself features ornate carvings, possibly inspired by classical or medieval designs, and is framed by a band of detailed stonework. The structure's surface is weathered, giving it an ancient, timeworn appearance, with a palette dominated by earthy tones of beige, gray, and brown. The rear and side views reveal the arch's depth and the solid, block-like construction of the wall and columns. This monument would serve as a striking architectural feature in a public square or at the entrance to a historic district.
description: A stone triumphal arch with ornate columns and detailed carvings, featuring a single arched passageway and a weathered, ancient appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full arched opening, decorative columns, and detailed stonework above the arch.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the arch and the layered stone construction, with the archway itself not visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the archway and columns from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the thickness and block structure of the arch.
